tasks.title: Make my_hubs the single source of truth for hub enumeration
tasks.description: Hub Explorer (and likely other pages) currently derives the hub list from route_aircraft, causing deleted hubs to remain visible if their extracted route data still exists. Fix by routing all hub enumeration through v_my_hubs.
tasks.details:
1. AUDIT every page/route/template that lists hubs: Hub Explorer, Buy Next dropdown, Global Buy Next, Fleet Health, Hub ROI Summary, extraction UI. Use grep: grep -rni 'DISTINCT.*origin_id\|DISTINCT.*hub' app/ templates/
2. Replace any 'SELECT DISTINCT origin_id FROM route_aircraft' or similar derivations with 'SELECT * FROM v_my_hubs WHERE is_active = 1'.
3. Add a delete_hub(airport_id) service in app/services/hubs.py that runs in one transaction: DELETE FROM route_aircraft WHERE origin_id=? OR dest_id=?; DELETE FROM route_aircraft_snapshot WHERE origin_id=? OR dest_id=?; DELETE FROM my_routes WHERE origin_id=? OR dest_id=?; DELETE FROM my_hubs WHERE airport_id=?; then VACUUM.
4. Wire the hub-delete UI button to delete_hub() instead of whatever partial delete it currently calls.
5. On 'add hub' insert with is_active=0; flip to 1 in the extraction job only after a successful run (set last_extract_status='success' and is_active=1 in the same UPDATE).
6. Update v_my_hubs filter usage in Hub Explorer to: WHERE is_active = 1 AND last_extract_status = 'success' — this ensures draft hubs and failed-extraction hubs don't appear.
tasks.testStrategy: Add a hub 'TST' via UI without extracting -> verify it does NOT appear in Hub Explorer (is_active=0). Run extraction for TST -> verify it appears. Click delete on TST -> verify zero rows in my_hubs, route_aircraft, route_aircraft_snapshot, my_routes for airport_id of TST. Restart app, confirm Hub Explorer shows only the 6 real hubs.

metadata.description: Fix ghost hub bug where deleted hubs remain visible in Hub Explorer due to leftover route_aircraft rows.
